name: Green Route of Almeida — Historic Villages of Portugal
name_original: Percurso verde de Almeida — Aldeias Históricas de Portugal
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Almeida traverse a landscape rich in history, characterized by fortified towns, rolling hills, and rural plains. The region features a mix of open countryside and routes connecting historic villages, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation changes are present, with some routes including moderate climbs and descents. The area's physical features include historical fortifications and agricultural land. Best touring cycling routes around Almeida The most popular touring cycling route is Walled Cities Trail (GR-80), a 68.6 miles (110.4 km) trail that takes 9 hours 20 minutes to complete, connecting several historic fortified towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Green Route of Almeida — Historic Villages of Portugal, a moderate 13.7 miles (22.0 km) path. This route winds through historic villages and rural landscapes, offering a scenic experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Fortress of Almeida – Castelo Rodrigo Village loop from Almeida, a 38.3 miles (61.7 km) trail leading through historic fortresses and villages, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Almeida is defined by historic fortifications, rural plains, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Almeida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 15 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almeida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almeida?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes around Almeida, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Almeida?
A: The routes traverse a landscape rich in history, characterized by fortified towns, rolling hills, and rural plains. You'll encounter a mix of open countryside and paths connecting historic villages, with some moderate climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Almeida?
A: Yes, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292958407/green-route-of-almeida-historic-villages-of-portugal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Route of Almeida — Historic Villages of Portugal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7 miles (22.0 km) path winding through historic villages and rural landscapes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32132619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida Castle – Historic Village of Almeida loop from Vilar Formoso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Almeida?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319354463/walled-cities-trail-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Cities Trail (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 68.6 miles (110.4 km) route connecting several historic fortified towns. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24545189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortress of Almeida – Castelo Rodrigo Village loop from Almeida&lt;/a&gt;, covering 38.3 miles (61.7 km).

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Almeida?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. You can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24545189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortress of Almeida – Castelo Rodrigo Village loop from Almeida&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24545186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida Castle – Castelo Rodrigo Village loop from Castelo Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32132619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida Castle – Historic Village of Almeida loop from Vilar Formoso&lt;/a&gt; is also a loop.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Almeida?
A: The routes often pass through areas rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore significant landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7842469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Village of Almeida&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Fuerte de la Concepción&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes connect historic villages and offer views of rural plains.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Almeida?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almeida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Cyclists often praise the historical significance of the routes and the varied terrain.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Almeida?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling in Almeida, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling trips in Almeida?
A: Yes, for a multi-day or longer trip, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319354463/walled-cities-trail-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Cities Trail (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 68.6 miles (110.4 km) route that connects several historic fortified towns, offering an immersive experience over a longer duration.

Q: Are there any specific historical sites to look out for while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its historical fortifications. Key sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7842469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Village of Almeida&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fort of the Concepción and Redoubt of San José&lt;/a&gt;. These are often integrated into the cycling routes, providing historical context to your ride.
tips:
Vale da Mula lies in a valley between the Aguiar and Tourões rivers, right next to the Spanish border. The current name comes from the adaptation of the Castilian “Vale de la Mula”, which was used until recently.

During the Restoration War, this discreet place played a prominent role and it was also from here that the Portuguese offensive towards Guardão began, the cradle of Spanish attacks on neighbouring lands.

The imposing fortress of Almeida that we enjoy today as a heritage tourist attraction was built in the 17th century. It is shaped like a 12-pointed star and is surrounded by a 12-meter-wide photos around the 2.5-kilometre perimeter. At its peak it was guarded by up to 5,000 men and was the scene of numerous battles.

Originally, in the 12th century, the town prospered around a castle that was part of the kingdom of León. Almeida became part of Portugal after the Treaty of Alcañices in 1297.
